one_time_description: Agricultural Benefits Specific Claim settlement from Canada under Treaty 8, awarded $134,879,644 with $83,055,134 disbursed as per capita distributions and trust contributions
economic_notes: Nation received $5,508,836 in additional COVID-19 funding from ISC. Driftpile Cree Nation Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust created to hold settlement proceeds for minor members. RBC bridge loan of $66,666,563 obtained to fund per capita distributions prior to receiving settlement funds, repaid in May 2021. Four business enterprises: Driftpile First Nation Enterprises Inc. (100%), Driftpile Cree Nation Limited Partnerships (99%), Driftpile Business Corporation (100%), with combined revenue of $9,380,816.
notes: Major one-time item: $134,879,644 Agricultural Benefits Specific Claim settlement received from Canada under Treaty 8, with $83,055,134 in related disbursements (per capita distributions and trust contributions) recorded as expenses. The Driftpile Cree Nation Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust is fully consolidated. Four GBEs accounted for under modified equity method. Settlement funds received subsequent to year end; balance presented net on balance sheet at year end. No schedule of remuneration provided. OSR calculation is approximate as CMHC subsidy and LSLIRC transfers are included in government transfers total of $27,060,663 (ISC $25,727,623 + LSLIRC $736,787 + CMHC $108,813 + FNDF $388,239 = $26,961,462; trust interest $24,502 + net entity income $2,374,255 and other income $3,468,440 treated as OSR).
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
